compiled_state.title: As a developer, implementation-plan assigns recipe-backed tasks where a deterministic transform is the better fit
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: /dso:implementation-plan reads recipe-registry.yaml during approach design and can assign a recipe: task type when a deterministic transform is a better fit than LLM generation.
**Why**: Mechanical transforms (add parameter, normalize imports) should be planned as deterministic tasks, not LLM-generated code. This enables the sprint to execute them via recipe-executor.sh instead of dispatching sub-agents.
**Scope**:
- IN: Implementation-plan reads recipe-registry.yaml, new recipe: task type in task decomposition, heuristic for when deterministic > LLM (based on recipe capability_description and scope fields), recipe task spec includes recipe name + parameters
- OUT: Sprint execution of recipe tasks (S2), cleanup (S3), pre-flight (S4), fallback (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, implementation-plan reads recipe-registry.yaml during approach design and recognizes available recipe capabilities
  ← Satisfies: "/dso:implementation-plan consults the recipe registry during approach design"
- When this story is complete, implementation-plan assigns recipe: task type for mechanical transforms (e.g., add-parameter signature change) while keeping business logic as LLM tasks, without splitting work artificially to maximize recipe usage
  ← Satisfies: "assigns recipe-backed tasks where a deterministic transform is a better fit than LLM generation"
- When this story is complete, recipe: task specs include the recipe name, parameters, and a natural-language intent description derived from the registry's capability_description field
  ← Satisfies: downstream S5 fallback needs natural-language intent for LLM translation
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] /dso:implementation-plan is a safeguard file — edits require explicit user approval per Never-Do rule 18
- [Testing] Heuristic criteria for "deterministic > LLM" must be testable — define clear conditions (e.g., recipe capability_description matches the task's mechanical action, task scope fits recipe's declared scope level)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
